title: portulaca oleracea
intro: Portulaca oleracea, also known as purslane, is a succulent annual plant that belongs to the Portulacaceae family. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Purslane is rich in vitamins A, C, and E, as well as minerals such as magnesium and iron, making it a nutritious addition to a healthy diet, with potential health benefits including reduced inflammation and improved heart health. Purslane can be consumed as a vegetable or used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be made by infusing the plant's leaves and stems in a carrier oil or solvent.
